function_v0.doc:
Executes a function call on another contract.

Requires authentication from the account itself.

# Arguments

* `target` - The address of the contract to call
* `target_fn` - The function name to call on the target contract
* `target_args` - The arguments to pass to the target function
function_v0.name: execute

function_v0.doc: Adds a policy contract to a context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: add_policy

function_v0.doc: Adds a new signer to a context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: add_signer

function_v0.doc:
Validates signatures against the account's context rules.

Verifies that the provided signatures match the required signers for the given execution context.

# Arguments

* `signature_payload` - The hash of the transaction data to verify
* `signatures` - The signatures to validate
* `auth_contexts` - The execution contexts for which authentication is required

# Returns

Returns `Ok(())` if authentication succeeds, or an error if validation fails.
function_v0.name: __check_auth

function_v0.doc: Removes a policy contract from a context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: remove_policy

function_v0.doc: Removes a signer from a context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: remove_signer

function_v0.doc: Creates a new context rule with specified signers and policies.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: add_context_rule

function_v0.doc: Retrieves a specific context rule by ID.
function_v0.name: get_context_rule

function_v0.doc: Removes a context rule from the account.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: remove_context_rule

function_v0.doc: Returns the total number of context rules.
function_v0.name: get_context_rules_count

function_v0.doc: Updates the name of an existing context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: update_context_rule_name

function_v0.doc: Updates the expiration time of a context rule.\n\nRequires authentication from the account itself.
function_v0.name: update_context_rule_valid_until

udt_union_v0.doc: Represents different types of signers in the smart account system.
udt_union_v0.name: Signer

tuple_v0.name: Delegated

tuple_v0.doc: An external signer with custom verification logic.\nContains the verifier contract address and the public key data.
tuple_v0.name: External

udt_struct_v0.doc:
The authorization payload passed to `__check_auth`, bundling cryptographic
proofs with context rule selection.

This struct carries two distinct pieces of information that are both
required for authorization but cannot be derived from each other:

- `signers` maps each [`Signer`] to its raw signature bytes, providing
cryptographic proof that the signer actually signed the transaction
payload. A context rule stores which signer *identities* are authorized
(via `signer_ids`), but the rule does not contain the signatures
themselves \xe2\x80\x94 those must be supplied here.

- `context_rule_ids` tells the system which rule to validate for each auth
context. Because multiple rules can exist for the same context type, the
caller must explicitly select one per context rather than relying on
auto-discovery. Each entry is aligned by index with the `auth_contexts`
passed to `__check_auth`.

The length of `context_rule_ids` must equal the number of auth contexts;
a mismatch is rejected with
[`SmartAccountError::ContextRuleIdsLen
udt_struct_v0.name: AuthPayload

fields.doc: Per-context rule IDs, aligned by index with `auth_contexts`.
fields.name: context_rule_ids

fields.doc: Signature data mapped to each signer.
fields.name: signers

WebAuthn signature data structure containing all components needed for

This structure encapsulates the signature and associated data generated
during a WebAuthn authentication ceremony.

fields.name: signature
